options: A shape that is very big
One half of a shape is the mirror image of the other half
A shape with straight lines only
questions.explanation: Symmetry means one half of a shape is the exact mirror image of the other half. When you fold the shape along the line of symmetry, both halves match perfectly.
hints: Think about a butterfly - what is special about its two wings?

questions.questionText: What is a line of symmetry?
options: Any line drawn on a shape
A line that divides a shape into two matching mirror halves
A curved line inside a circle
The longest line of a shape
questions.explanation: A line of symmetry is a line that divides a shape into two equal halves that are mirror images of each other. If you fold along this line, both halves overlap exactly.
hints: Think about folding a shape - when do both halves match?

questions.questionText: How many lines of symmetry does a square have?
questions.explanation: A square has 4 lines of symmetry: one horizontal, one vertical, and two diagonal lines. You can fold a square along any of these 4 lines and both halves will match.
hints: Think about all the ways you can fold a square so both halves match

questions.questionText: True or False: A rectangle has 4 lines of symmetry, just like a square.
questions.explanation: False! A rectangle has only 2 lines of symmetry (horizontal and vertical). Its diagonals are NOT lines of symmetry because if you fold along a diagonal, the halves do not match. A square has 4 lines of symmetry.
hints: Try folding a rectangle along its diagonal - do both halves match?

questions.questionText: Which of these objects in nature has symmetry?
questions.explanation: A butterfly has symmetry! Its left wing is the mirror image of its right wing. Clouds, tree trunks, and rivers usually do not have a clear line of symmetry.
hints: Which of these looks the same on both sides?

questions.questionText: In a mirror image, what happens to the left and right sides?
questions.explanation: In a mirror image, left and right sides are swapped. If you raise your right hand in front of a mirror, it looks like the mirror image is raising the left hand.
hints: Stand in front of a mirror and raise one hand - which hand does the reflection raise?

questions.questionText: Which English letter has a vertical line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: The letter A has a vertical line of symmetry. If you fold A along a vertical line through its middle, the left half mirrors the right half. F, G, and J have no line of symmetry.
hints: Which letter looks the same on the left and right sides?

questions.questionText: How can you check if a shape has a line of symmetry?
options: Fold it and see if both halves match
questions.explanation: The best way to check is the folding test. Fold the shape along the line - if both halves overlap and match exactly, the line is a line of symmetry.
hints: What simple test tells you if both halves are matching?

questions.questionText: How many lines of symmetry does a circle have?
questions.explanation: A circle has unlimited (infinite) lines of symmetry! Any straight line that passes through the centre of the circle divides it into two matching halves.
hints: Can you draw a line through the centre of a circle at any angle?

questions.questionText: When you fold a paper in half and cut a shape, what happens when you open it?
options: You get a symmetrical shape with the fold as the line of symmetry
questions.explanation: When you fold paper in half, cut a shape along the fold, and open it, you get a symmetrical shape. The fold line becomes the line of symmetry and both halves are mirror images.
hints: Think of making paper hearts or paper snowflakes

questions.questionText: True or False: The letter O has both horizontal and vertical lines of symmetry.
questions.explanation: True! The letter O has both a horizontal line of symmetry (top and bottom halves match) and a vertical line of symmetry (left and right halves match).
hints: Try folding the letter O both ways

questions.questionText: An equilateral triangle (all sides equal) has how many lines of symmetry?
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has 3 lines of symmetry. Each line goes from one corner to the middle of the opposite side. All three sides are equal, so there are three ways to fold it into matching halves.
hints: Each line goes from a corner to the middle of the opposite side

questions.questionText: What is a shape with no line of symmetry called?
questions.explanation: A shape with no line of symmetry is called asymmetrical. No matter how you try to fold it, the two halves will never match exactly.

questions.questionText: Where would you see symmetry in Indian architecture?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal is a famous example of symmetry in Indian architecture. When viewed from the front, the left side is the mirror image of the right side. It has a vertical line of symmetry.
hints: Think of a famous white marble building in Agra

questions.questionText: To complete a symmetric figure on grid paper, each point on the missing half should be at the _____ distance from the line of symmetry as the matching point on the given half.
questions.explanation: Each point on the missing half should be at the same distance from the line of symmetry as the corresponding point on the given half. This is what makes both halves mirror images.
hints: In a mirror, is your reflection closer or further from the mirror than you?

questions.questionText: True or False: A leaf has a line of symmetry along its midrib.
questions.explanation: True! Most leaves have a line of symmetry along the midrib (the central vein). The left half of the leaf is roughly the mirror image of the right half.
hints: Look at the central line running through a leaf

questions.questionText: If you fold a paper in half twice and then cut a small triangle near the corner, how many triangle holes will appear when you unfold the paper completely?
questions.explanation: You will see 4 triangle holes. Each fold doubles the number of cuts. First fold doubles 1 to 2, second fold doubles 2 to 4. This is why paper snowflakes have repeating patterns!
hints: Each fold doubles the number of cuts

questions.questionText: Which of these shapes has exactly 1 line of symmetry?
questions.explanation: An isosceles triangle (with two equal sides) has exactly 1 line of symmetry - the line from the top vertex to the middle of the base. A square has 4, a circle has unlimited, and a rectangle has 2 lines of symmetry.
hints: Which shape can be folded into matching halves in only one way?
